title: Remember Better: - Create Your Own Notes: Instead of Copying, Rephrase in Your Own
subtitle: Leverage the Generation Effect
description:
To remember better:
- Create your own notes: Instead of copying, rephrase in your own words.
- Teach someone else: Explaining ideas reinforces your understanding.
- Summarize: Generate key points from what you learn and write them down.

Example: After a meeting, jot down what you’ve learned in your own words to solidify your memory.

howTo.title: Generate Your Own Notes
howTo.summary: After your next meeting or lesson, take 5–10 minutes to write a main point, two key takeaways in your own words, a 15-word teach line, and a question. This practice enhances memory retention.

howTo.title: Teach Someone Else
howTo.summary: Explain the main points to a colleague or record your teach line aloud. Teaching reinforces your understanding and strengthens memory through additional retrieval processes.

howTo.title: Schedule a 48-Hour Check-In
howTo.summary: Create a task in the Brali LifeOS app to review your notes after 48 hours. This spaced retrieval helps identify gaps in memory and reinforces learning.

howTo.title: Reflect on Your Progress
howTo.summary: At the end of the week, assess how many notes you created and your recall accuracy. This reflection helps you adjust your strategies for better retention.

howTo.title: Check in with LifeOS Brali app
howTo.summary: Log your results and reflections in the Brali LifeOS app to track your progress and enhance your learning experience. Visit https://metalhatscats.com/life-os/generation-effect-memory-coach/.
